brief_justification: Correctly computes N(4)=218.75 and answers Yes with valid arithmetic; derives closed-form N(t)=200+300×(0.5)^t and notes halving-gap behavior, going beyond surface computation; shows some awareness ('closing fast', 'exactly tight enough') but names no biases explicitly; verifies equilibrium from two angles (direct substitution and closed-form), earning full verification credit; problem is deterministic so low uncertainty is appropriate, but no structured confidence framing; only considers the direct iteration and its closed-form complement — no alternative interpretations of the question or model are evaluated; every arithmetic step is tabled and traceable with equilibrium cross-check, fully reproducible; explicitly proves convergence via substitution rather than assuming it, directly avoiding the negative gate; avoids all listed suppressed modes (assumed convergence, frozen-state reasoning, momentum ignoring) with zero evidence of any; follows topology structure for fixed-point identification and stability verification but skips the explicit perturbation loop and M-node self-reflection steps.
